preferred.relationship_context: married or in a stable partnership
preferred.social_context: still actively maintains friend groups but dislikes becoming the full-time organizer
preferred.work_context: busy professional with limited planning bandwidth
core_attraction: share calendar and vote can reduce WhatsApp back-and-forth
booking and payment are contained in one flow
reduces awkwardness around who pays first and who has confirmed
core_objections: friends may not want another app
calendar sync or voting may become more work if unreliable
split payment and refund policy may create social friction
recommendation may optimize for one person instead of the group
questions_to_surface: Can people join the planning flow without downloading the app?
What happens when someone replies late or drops out?
Do I need to front the full payment?
Is calendar sharing read-only or can it affect my schedule?
preferred.recommendation_reaction_bias: cares whether recommendations actually help group choice rather than only personal preference
preferred.retention_hypothesis: will retain only if the platform reliably lowers coordination burden across repeated social planning moments
